overview: The Marathon County Health & Human Services Committee held an educational orientation meeting for new and returning members, featuring presentations from department heads on the Health Department, Social Services, Veteran Service Office, and Aging Disability Resource Center. No action items were voted on beyond approving April 1st meeting minutes; the meeting focused on familiarizing committee members with departmental missions, budgets, and services.
committee: Marathon County Health & Human Services Committee

agenda.item: Call to order and remote participation confirmation
Approval of April 1st committee meeting minutes
Review of operational functions required by statute
Review of standing committee ordinance section 2.04 - missions, duties, responsibilities
Aging Disability Resource Center (ADRC) overview presentation
Next meeting date confirmation and adjournment

discussions.body: Supervisor Cavelli made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Vice Chair Rosenberg. The motion carried unanimously with all in favor.

Administrator Lance Leonard provided an overview of the committee's jurisdiction under section 2.04 sub 5, explaining reporting relationships with departments (Health, Social Services, Veterans), multi-county entities (North Central Health Care, ADRC), and subordinate boards (Social Services Board, Veteran Service Commission, Board of Health). Chair Boots noted the committee will focus on developing and monitoring the new strategic plan through December.

Health Officer Caitlyn Suginaka presented the department's mission of preventing disease, promoting health, and protecting from environmental hazards. She highlighted over 1,200 licenses overseen, public health vending machines stocked with free resources including tick kits, the Nurse Family Partnership program graduating its first two-year-olds, and a new homeless system response position funded through the county and City of Wausau partnership. The department has approximately 37.6 FTE with about half of its $5 million budget from tax levy.

Administrator Leonard presented on behalf of Director Christa, describing the department's divisions including child welfare, youth justice, children's long-term support, economic support, and child support. He highlighted the successful Family Keys pilot program addressing housing instability, which was substantially modified based on board feedback. The department has about 140 FTE with a $22 million budget, of which approximately $7 million is tax levy.

Veteran Service Officer Aaron Galindo reported the department grew to 3.0 FTE and had 8,149 client interactions in 2025. He highlighted $44 million annually in benefits flowing to Marathon County veterans, with $2.6 million in retroactive payments in 2025 alone. Key initiatives include surviving spouse benefits outreach, housing stability partnerships with Center for Veterans Issues and HUDVASH, and suicide prevention efforts noting 7 of 30 county suicides in 2025 were veterans. Supervisor Kremes asked about staffing adequacy; Galindo acknowledged they don't have enough time but are managing well.

Executive Director Mike Ray explained the ADRC serves four counties (Marathon, Lincoln, Langlade, Wood) as a one-stop shop for aging and disability services. He described significant financial challenges with nutrition program costs up 40-45% over four years while federal funding remained flat. The organization has about 60 FTEs plus 385-400 volunteers. Vice Chair Rosenberg praised the culturally appropriate nutrition program at the Hmong American Center. Ray noted the ADRC hadn't requested a levy increase from 2011 until 2024.
Vice Chair Rosenberg caught an error in the announced next meeting date. The committee confirmed the next meeting will be Tuesday, June 3rd at 3 PM, not May 29th as initially stated.

actionItems: North Central Healthcare to be scheduled for a separate full presentation before the committee
ADRC Executive Director to present annual report with 2025 data to full board
Chair Boots to bring strategic plan discussion items to upcoming meetings
Committee members to email Chair Boots and Administrator Leonard with agenda item suggestions
Next meeting scheduled for Tuesday, June 3rd at 3 PM
title: Marathon County Health & Human Services Committee Meeting
